title: "Cryo-EM structure of full-length α-synuclein amyloid fibril with Parkinson’s disease familial A53T mutation | Cell Research"

mime: "text/html"
converter: biorouter-knowledge
---

*   Letter to the Editor
*   Published: 12 March 2020

# Cryo-EM structure of full-length α-synuclein amyloid fibril with Parkinson’s disease familial A53T mutation

*   [Yunpeng Sun](#auth-Yunpeng-Sun-Aff1-Aff2)[1](#Aff1),[2](#Aff2) [na1](#na1),
*   [Shouqiao Hou](#auth-Shouqiao-Hou-Aff1-Aff2)[1](#Aff1),[2](#Aff2) [na1](#na1),
*   [Kun Zhao](#auth-Kun-Zhao-Aff1-Aff2)[1](#Aff1),[2](#Aff2) [na1](#na1),
*   [Houfang Long](#auth-Houfang-Long-Aff1-Aff2)[1](#Aff1),[2](#Aff2),
*   [Zhenying Liu](#auth-Zhenying-Liu-Aff1-Aff2)[1](#Aff1),[2](#Aff2),
*   [Jing Gao](#auth-Jing-Gao-Aff1-Aff2)[1](#Aff1),[2](#Aff2),
*   [Yaoyang Zhang](#auth-Yaoyang-Zhang-Aff1-Aff2)  [ORCID: orcid.org/0000-0001-5363-9834](https://orcid.org/0000-0001-5363-9834)[1](#Aff1),[2](#Aff2),
*   [Xiao-Dong Su](#auth-Xiao_Dong-Su-Aff3)[3](#Aff3),
*   [Dan Li](#auth-Dan-Li-Aff4-Aff5)[4](#Aff4),[5](#Aff5) &
*   …
*   [Cong Liu](#auth-Cong-Liu-Aff1-Aff2)  [ORCID: orcid.org/0000-0003-3425-6672](https://orcid.org/0000-0003-3425-6672)[1](#Aff1),[2](#Aff2)

### Subjects

*   [Cryoelectron microscopy](/subjects/cryoelectron-microscopy)
*   [Mechanisms of disease](/subjects/mechanisms-of-disease)

Dear Editor,

α-Synuclein (α-Syn) forms amyloid fibrils accumulating in Lewy bodies (LB) and Lewy neuritis (LN), which is a common histological hallmark of Parkinson’s disease (PD) and other synucleinopathies.[1](/articles/s41422-020-0299-4#ref-CR1 "Goedert, M., Spillantini, M. G., Del Tredici, K. & Braak, H. Nat. Rev. Neurol. 9, 13–24 (2013).") Moreover, α-syn amyloid fibrils spread in the patient’s brain via cell-to-cell transmission, which accounts for the disease progression.[2](/articles/s41422-020-0299-4#ref-CR2 "Luk, K. C. et al. Science 338, 949–953 (2012).") Several single amino-acid mutations of α-syn, including A53T, E46K, H50Q, G51D, A30P and A53E, have been identified from familial PD patients, which are causative to the early-onset pathology with different clinical symptoms.[3](/articles/s41422-020-0299-4#ref-CR3 "Rosborough, K., Patel, N. & Kalia, L. V. Curr. Neurol. Neurosci. Rep. 17, 31 (2017).") Among them, A53T is the first hereditary mutation of α-syn discovered in Italian and Greek families with autosomal dominant and early-onset PD.[4](/articles/s41422-020-0299-4#ref-CR4 "Polymeropoulos, M. H. et al. Science 276, 2045–2047 (1997).") Till now, A53T represents the most commonly reported cases of familial PD involving more than 10 families of Greek, Korean, Swedish and Chinese origin.[3](/articles/s41422-020-0299-4#ref-CR3 "Rosborough, K., Patel, N. & Kalia, L. V. Curr. Neurol. Neurosci. Rep. 17, 31 (2017).") A53T mutation promotes α-syn fibril formation in vitro, and exhibits exacerbated PD-like pathology both in cellular and animal models.[5](/articles/s41422-020-0299-4#ref-CR5 "Giasson, B. I. et al. Neuron 34, 521–533 (2002)."),[6](/articles/s41422-020-0299-4#ref-CR6 "Narhi, L. et al. J. Biol. Chem. 274, 9843–9846 (1999).") Previous structural studies on the wild-type (WT) α-syn fibril reveal that A53 is one of the key residues in the interface of α-syn protofilaments.[7](#ref-CR7 "Li, Y. et al. Cell Res. 28, 897–903 (2018)."),[8](#ref-CR8 "Li, B. et al. Nat. Commun. 9, 3609 (2018)."),[9](/articles/s41422-020-0299-4#ref-CR9 "Guerrero-Ferreira, R. et al. eLife 7, e36402 (2018).") However, it remains unclear how the A53T mutation may alter the fibril structure and exacerbate α-syn pathology.

## Acknowledgements

We thank the cryo-EM platform of Peking University for help with data collection. This work was supported by the Major State Basic Research Development Program (2016YFA0501902), the National Natural Science Foundation (NSF) of China (91853113 and 31872716), the Science and Technology Commission of Shanghai Municipality (18JC1420500), “Eastern Scholar” project supported by Shanghai Municipal Education Commission, the Shanghai Pujiang Program (18PJ1404300), the Shanghai Municipal Science and Technology Major Project (Grant No. 2019SHZDZX02). ### Contributions

C.L., K.Z and D.L. designed the project. K.Z and Z.L prepared the fibril samples. Y.S., K.Z and X.S. performed the Cryo-EM experiments and determined the structure of fibrils. S.H. and H.L performed the cellular assays. J.G. and Y.Z performed the mass spectrum-based experiment. All of the authors are involved in analyzing the data and contributed to manuscript discussion and editing. D.L. and C.L. wrote the manuscript.

### Corresponding authors

Correspondence to [Dan Li](mailto:lidan2017@sjtu.edu.cn) or [Cong Liu](mailto:liulab@sioc.ac.cn).
